title: Steel Making
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module introduces the steel making process in stainless steel manufacturing. It explains how raw materials are converted into molten stainless steel using electric furnaces and refining processes before casting.
learningObjectives: Understand the complete steel making process.
Identify the major stages of steel production.
Learn the role of each furnace used in steel making.
Understand quality control during steel production.
Recognize the importance of process monitoring.

content.introduction: Steel making is the core manufacturing process where stainless steel is produced by melting raw materials and refining the molten metal to achieve the required chemical composition and quality standards.
content.overview: The steel making shop converts scrap and alloy materials into molten stainless steel through a sequence of melting, refining, composition adjustment, and casting operations.
safetyPrecautions: Maintain safe distance from molten steel.
content.summary: Steel making is a controlled manufacturing process where molten stainless steel is produced through melting, refining, alloy adjustment, and quality control before it is sent for continuous casting.
